[Image of pathology slide]

Pathologic diagnosis:
Sclera, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Inflammatory pseudotumor

Microscopic findings:
Sections show a well-demarcated mass composed of spindle cells and fibroblasts with a storiform pattern. The tumor cells are arranged in fascicles and interlacing bundles. There is a marked lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate within the tumor stroma. The tumor cells are positive for vimentin and CD34, and negative for S100, HMB45, and Melan-A. The Ki67 labeling index is low (approximately 2-3%). No evidence of atypia or mitotic activity is noted. The tumor lacks any evidence of melanocytic differentiation. The inflammatory component is composed of lymphocytes, plasma cells, and histiocytes. No evidence of malignancy is noted.
==

Radial spread: 0

**4th case:**

Pathologic diagnosis:
Conjunctiva, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Conjunctival intraepithelial neoplasia (CIN) grade 2

Microscopic findings:
Sections show a well-demarcated area of dysplasia involving the superficial and mid-epithelial layers. The dysplastic cells exhibit hyperchromatic nuclei with increased nuclear-cytoplasmic ratio and pleomorphism. There is focal loss of cell polarity and disorganization of the epithelial architecture. The dysplastic cells are positive for p63 and negative for p16. Ki67 labeling index is approximately 10%. No evidence of invasion into the stroma or basement membrane is noted. The surrounding conjunctival tissue appears normal.

First give one or two tentative pathology diagnosis, 
then describe the related microscopic findings:

---

Pathologic diagnosis:
Sclera, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Inflammatory pseudotumor

Microscopic findings:
Sections of the scleral tissue demonstrate a predominantly mononuclear inflammatory infiltrate composed of lymphocytes and plasma cells. The infiltrate is distributed in a perivascular and interstitial pattern. There is no evidence of neoplastic cells or significant atypia. The stroma shows mild edema and fibrosis. No vasculitis or granulomatous inflammation is observed. Immunohistochemical staining for CD3 highlights the lymphocytic component, while CD20 and CD138 are positive for the plasma cells. The absence of Bcl-2 and Ki67 proliferation index <1% suggests a benign process. The overall histological features are consistent with an inflammatory pseudotumor.
==
Proposed WHO grading system: Benign
==

Pathologic diagnosis:
Sclera, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Scleral fibrosis

Microscopic findings:
Sections show scleral tissue with features of fibrosis. The collagen bundles are thickened and irregularly arranged, with increased interstitial fibroblasts and myofibroblasts. There is no evidence of inflammation or neoplastic cells. The fibrotic changes are consistent with a chronic process. The tissue is positive for α-SMA and vimentin, indicating the presence of myofibroblasts. No evidence of active inflammation or malignancy is noted.
==
Proposed WHO grading system: Grade 1 (mild)
==

Case 5:
Pathologic diagnosis:
Retina, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Retinal detachment with proliferative vitreoretinopathy

Microscopic findings:
Sections show a retinal detachment with proliferative vitreoretinopathy (PVR). The retina is separated from the underlying choroid by a fibrocellular membrane. The detached retina exhibits atrophy and gliosis. The vitreous cavity contains a dense, fibrous proliferation of cells, which are predominantly fibroblasts and myofibroblasts. The cells are positive for vimentin and alpha-smooth muscle actin (α-SMA). The retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) shows hyperplasia and pigmentation. The RPE layer is discontinuous in some areas. The choroid is intact without evidence of neovascularization. No evidence of malignancy or inflammatory infiltrate is noted.
==

---

First give one or two tentative pathology diagnosis, 
then describe the related microscopic findings:

---

Pathologic diagnosis:
Sclera, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Inflammatory pseudotumor

Microscopic findings:
Sections of the scleral tissue demonstrate a predominantly mononuclear inflammatory infiltrate composed of lymphocytes and plasma cells. The infiltrate is distributed in a perivascular and interstitial pattern. Focal areas of fibroblastic proliferation are noted, with some collagen deposition. No evidence of neoplastic cells is identified. The inflammatory cells are positive for CD3 and CD20. The presence of eosinophils and neutrophils is also observed. No significant vasculitis or granulomatous reaction is present. The overall architecture of the sclera remains intact, with preserved lamellar organization.

Pathologic diagnosis:
Sclera, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Inflammatory pseudotumor

Microscopic findings:
Sections of the scleral tissue demonstrate an inflammatory pseudotumor. The lesion is composed of a mixed population of inflammatory cells including lymphocytes, plasma cells, and histiocytes in a fibrovascular stroma. There is no evidence of neoplastic or infectious etiology. The inflammatory infiltrate is predominantly lymphocytic with scattered plasma cells and histiocytes. **New Case:**

Pathologic diagnosis:
Conjunctiva, OD, excisional biopsy
---Intraepithelial nevus

Microscopic findings:
Sections of the conjunctival specimen show intraepithelial nevus. The lesion is composed of a proliferation of melanocytic cells within the epithelium. The melanocytes are arranged in nests and cords, with some areas showing mild atypia. The nuclei of the melanocytes are slightly enlarged but not markedly so. The cells are positive for SOX-10 and HMB45. No significant lymphocytic infiltration or other inflammatory changes are noted. The Ki67 labeling index is low (approximately 2%). No evidence of vertical or radial spread is observed.

---

Melanocytic atypia: 1
==

**Pathologic diagnosis:**
Macula, OD, excisional biopsy
--- Choroidal nevus

Microscopic findings:
Sections of the specimen show a well-demarcated lesion composed of nests and sheets of melanocytes in the choroid. The nevus cells are arranged in a radial pattern with a central stromal core. The nevus cells are positive for HMB45 and SOX-10. No atypia or mitotic activity is observed. The lesion is surrounded by a thin layer of inflammatory cells, predominantly lymphocytes. No evidence of invasion into the overlying retinal layers or underlying sclera is noted. The BRAF staining is negative.
